Segmentation and Confirmation (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 4
Speaker 1: Hey guys welcome to module three. In this lesson we're gonna be talking about the
segmentation and the confirmation subsequences of the overall framework. Here it is
on our map, we've got the beginning of our journey and really what I would consider the
two most important subsections of your launch, this is what's really going to set the
foundation and ignite your customer's journey, you need to set them on that path
towards becoming a buyer.
As we learned earlier just because an email or a sequence doesn't link to a sales page
doesn't mean that it's not transactional, it just means that things we're transacting or in
this case coaching are more subtle, more subcutaneous and beneath the surface. Unless
we start our campaign with these little micro conversions we're gonna have a huge,
huge problem trying to get the conversion rates down the road.
With that in mind what is the segmentation and confirmation coaching? The first thing
should be obvious, it's self selection. If we expect someone to embark on a journey they
definitely need to enlist, they need to volunteer, they need to raise their hand and say,
"Yes I am in," and that's why I recommend that you send a segmentation or call out
email even if you plan to send your offer and follow up emails to your entire list because
just the act of getting someone to buy in, to raise their hand, to have that micro
commitment is a very important precursor for the later conversion, so don't skip it.
What is it that gets people to raise their hand? Really it comes down to excitement
about the new outcome and that's a given, no one's gonna come along for a ride if they
don't care about the destination. Further, we really need our prospect to get a hint that
the ride is actually gonna get them there, get them to where they need to go so it needs
to cut through their disillusionment just enough to get them excited. They don't need to
know the how right away, that comes later in fact it's important at this stage that they
don't know about the offer because that's only gonna add friction to the excitement,
but they do need to feel excited and hopeful enough to move on to the next step.
One of the things that gets people excited actually has nothing to do with you and
everything to do with them and that's what I call exclusive empowerment. You know
this is where we're giving them a chance to cash in on their privileged points, on their
status, give them a chance to leverage their status, their power, their achievements,
their circumstances, and all those things that are prerequisites for coming along on the
ride. We want to trigger that pride around what they've either accomplished or endured
and when you think about it this really is a powerful but advanced form of loss aversion.
If we're not making the most out of our unique advantages and opportunities we feel
like we're losing the game so this is really giving them a chance to cash in on that. Even
if they're not fully sold on the possibility of the outcome yet just the fact that they're in
a position of exclusive empowerment to pursue it is often enough to get someone
onboard and excited. This is a very powerful trigger and it's worth just taking a look at
some of the sources of exclusive empowerment.
Now you could start from a place of previous distinctions and accomplishments. If
they're an author of a best selling book, if they already speak basic Italian, if they
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

Segmentation and Confirmation (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 4
already have a successful six figure online business or possess certain certifications or
designations you know taking the maybe 101 version of your program all of those are
very tangible, very concrete examples of what could exclusively empower them to come
on this journey. You could also be more situational and circumstantial, if you're the
father of a newborn child, if you work remotely, if you have 10 hours per week to
commit to losing weight for example, if you've recently been laid off from a corporate
job that could be reframed as a positive opportunity. Those are circumstantial, it could
even be geographical, we're looking for nurses in Montreal to go to Ottawa for example.
Anything that is situation and circumstantial could also be used as a source of exclusive
empowerment.
Then finally probably the most powerful one here is things they've endured. People are
very proud of their battle scars sometimes, they really want to add meaning to things
they've endured in the past, the things they've overcome, that's probably the thing we
feel the most pride around and something that I invite you to try to see if you can tap
into. An example of this would be you've already gone through the withdrawal phase of
caffeine addiction or recovered from a cancer diagnosis or rebounded from bankruptcy
so anything that is a trial or tribulation and something they've overcome there's gonna
be a lot of pride around that and it's a prime opportunity for you to make that part of
the entry ticket of getting into you funnel, going along your buyers journey.
That is exclusive empowerment and just to give you an example of how powerful this
can be and how it can really shape what a prospect is motivated to do and not
motivated to do, a few years ago when I was still a screenwriter and really looking for
what would bring in the income I was considering law school. I went through the LSATs
which is kind of the entry exams and I applied kind of begrudgingly, I didn't really want
to go to law school it was kind of a family pressure thing met with how am I gonna make
money thing. I kind of just road out that process, I submitted, I applied to a few things,
and I got a letter at one point. I got a letter from I think it was the University of Ottawa.
I was living in Montreal at the time which is primarily a French speaking place and they
told me that because I live in Montreal, because I presumably speak French, which I
don't, they would be happy to welcome me into a special French version of their
program, they were really trying to recruit some bilingual people to come study in that
program. Even though I had really no interest in being a lawyer, let alone taking that
program in French which I don't even speak, or moving to Ottawa which I would not
want to do, no offense to people who live in Ottawa I was very close to doing it. I had
my exclusive empowerment goggles on and it took kind of a final hour withdrawal and a
shot of whiskey with a really good friend to talk me out of it. That's how powerful this
trigger of exclusive empowerment can be.
Finally the last thing we're coaching into sequence is steadfast, friction free
commitment to the outcome or what I call opening up the consistency loop so we can
close it later in the sales and tighten it in the closing sequence. Because attention moves
so fast we rarely, if ever, stop to add meaning behind the actions we take online and it's
up to you as a conversion coach to add layers of meaning behind that click. You need to
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

Segmentation and Confirmation (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 3 of 4
take a semi benign action such as an opt in, an email sign up, and turn that into a
steadfast commitment to the outcome and that's really the role of your confirmation.
It's important to get this commitment at this stage because it's the most friction free
environment of your funnel and that's when we want to get buy in, we want to get buy
in in that friction free state where you audience, where your prospect is most receptive,
their attention's there, they've just opted in. This is what's gonna give us a chance to
hold them accountable to that outcome later which I call closing that consistency loop.
You get that buy in in a place of lower resistance when there's no offer, there's no
friction, and then you hold them accountable to it later once it gets re-contextualized
with your offer. The same goes with excitement in that outcome, it's a lot easier to get
excited when your product isn't in the mix, when there's no friction, the stakes are
lower. It's easier to commit to that outcome, it's easier to commit to the excitement
around getting on that journey when that friction of the offer isn't there. This is a risk
free transaction of desire before we tie it in to an investment later. That's really what
we're doing, we're getting buy in without the price tag.
This is really kind of the enduring point I want to leave you with as far as what we're
coaching, just getting your prospect to say yes to the outcome in that friction free
environment. In doing so we inherit the right to keep them consistent with it later on in
the sales sequence when it is tied to an offer. That's when we're gonna close the
consistency loop, that's where we're gonna enact our FOMO for them as a coach and
really challenge them and hold their feet to the fire and say, "I thought you wanted this?
You know you told me that you were ready to commit to this," and it's not manipulative,
it's really helping them get out of their own way, it's helping them get through that
resistance, it's helping them see what they've committed to and holding them
accountable to that because prospects do have responsibilities too, our leads have
responsibilities, and that responsibility that we're holding them to is really staying
consistent.
That's the major point here, it's adding that meaning behind the click, the confirmation
email is probably the most wasted opportunity in any funnel. That's when we really
want to reflect back to them, what's probably been going on in their lives that led them
to clicking and joining and really creating that commitment, showing them what they're
committed to based on that action, that's what is diagnostic, diagnosing the meaning
behind the click and you're gonna see some theme plates on how to do that in just a
second.
The last thing I kind of want to touch upon is that segmentation can be naturally
occurring. If you already have someone on that segmented list either because they
came in through an ad or through partner traffic then you don't have to send the
segmentation call out email that we're gonna explore next, however it's still going to be
a good idea to really watch it and go through those exercises so you could see the
principles at play and really it could be an opportunity to integrate that into your landing
page. Regardless of whether or not you're gonna be sending out a segmentation call out
email I don't want you to miss out the opportunity to at least absorb those ingredients
and at the very least, at the very least explore the confirmation email which is where
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

Segmentation and Confirmation (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 4 of 4
we're gonna be creating that consistency, you'll be reflecting back, and diagnosing what
brought them here and reflecting back that meaning so we could hold them accountable
to it later.
That is it, once again confirmation email most under leveraged in online marketing, it's
the one that doesn't get the love it deserves. It's gonna be your most highly opened
email, generally speaking more people are gonna open that than anything else and it's
your best opportunity to really reflect back that meaning. We're gonna learn how to do
that in detail when we do the walkthroughs of the what this says about you theme
plate. This is usually my starting point when optimizing campaigns for clients and it's
crazy how much of an impact a well crafted confirmation email can make.
On that note I'm gonna duck out, it's time to start putting in the work so if you haven't
already done so go ahead, download the workbook for this module, and meet me in the
next section where we're gonna do a walkthrough on that and explore the theme plates.
I will see you in the next video.
